channel.title: Canned Publishing – English
channel.description: Svensk politik och nyheter, flerspråkigt och i klarspråk.

item.title: Technical committees, September minutes, Svedala municipality, reference numbers 8385-87-88.
item.description: The Technical Committee in Svedala has decided not to install a drinking water supply at the Holmeja playground because it is too expensive. They have also made changes to the budget for water and sewage services.

item.title: IT Department, Älvkarleby Municipality November 22, 2044
item.description: SAMMANTRÄDESPROTOKOLL IT-nämnden 2024-11-22 1 Tid och plats 22 november 2024, kl. 15:00 - 15:45, digitalt sammanträde Paragrafer § 21 - § 30 Justering Protokollet justeras digitalt av ordförande, Sara

item.title: Markets today: OMXS30
item.description: Stockholm's large-cap index OMXS30 is at 3 181 (+0.1%) versus the previous trading day.

item.title: The ECB (European Central Bank) reports on the progress made towards the introduction of the euro.
item.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) reports that countries seeking to adopt the euro are facing difficulties in meeting the necessary criteria. Wars and global uncertainty are hindering the economic development of these countries, preventing th

item.title: Iceland joins TIPS for instant payments.
item.description: Island has signed an agreement that will allow for the direct transfer of funds in Icelandic krona, available 24/7. This will become effective in 2028 through a new system for fast payments.

item.title: Statements from Commissioner Hoekstra regarding the proposal for simplified tax rules.
item.description: The European Commission aims to make it easier for businesses to operate by simplifying tax regulations.

item.title: Remarks by the Executive Vice-President Virkkunen and Commissioners Brunner and McGrath regarding the strengthening of Europol and Eurojust.
item.description: The EU has decided on new regulations to strengthen the authorities Europol and Eurojust. This is intended to help combat crime in Europe more effectively.

item.title: Daily News, 2026-06-24.
item.description: The EU plans to borrow up to 80 billion euros during the second half of 2026 by issuing new bonds, known as EU bonds.

item.title: The European Commission will disburse 846 million euros to Spain to support the recovery efforts following the floods in the Valencia region in 2024.
item.description: The European Union is providing €846 million to Spain to help rebuild the Valencia region following the major floods of 2024.

item.title: The European Union and Benin are strengthening their partnership in areas such as economy, security, and sustainability.
item.description: The European Union and the country of Benin will increase cooperation in the areas of economy, security, and the environment. The goal is to create a secure environment for long-term investments between the two parties.

item.title: Video message from President von der Leyen at Decarbon Days in Cottbus.
item.description: On June 24, 2026, Ursula von der Leyen, President of the European Commission, delivered a speech at an event called "Decarbon Days" in Cottbus. The speech focused on efforts to reduce carbon dioxide emissions.

item.title: Neighbor Consultation: SKROMBERGA 12 14, Bjuv Municipality, 2026-06-24.
item.description: Neighbors of the property located at Skromberga 12:14 in Bjuv have been given the opportunity to express their opinions on a proposal to change the intended use of a building. This is because the area does not yet have a finalized zoning pl

item.title: Education and Culture Committee Working Group, Markaryd Municipality, Allocation, June 24, 2026.
item.description: This document serves only as a notification that a meeting has taken place and that the decisions recorded in the minutes have been approved. No new decisions regarding schools or cultural activities were made in this specific text.

item.title: Social Services Department, Älmhult Municipality, June 24, 2026.
item.description: The social welfare board in Älmhult has reviewed financial reports and is preparing for new laws that will come into effect in July 2026. They have also been examining the budget for the future.

item.title: Urban Planning Committee, Aneby Municipality, June 24, 2026.
item.description: The Planning and Building Committee in Aneby has approved a new plan to build six houses in the Skidbacken area. The committee has also approved the financial report for May.

item.title: Neighbor Consultation: SKROMBERGA, Municipality of Bjuv, 2026-06-24.
item.description: Someone wants to change how a building at Skromberga 12:14 is used. Because there isn't a pre-existing plan for the area, the people who live nearby have the opportunity to express their opinions about the proposal.

item.title: ON, Hedemora Municipality, June 24, 2026.
item.description: The Social Care Committee of Hedemora held a meeting on June 24, 2026, to make decisions on important issues within healthcare and social care, including finances, staffing, and future plans for elderly care.

item.title: Municipal Council, Hällefors Municipality, June 24, 2026.
item.description: The municipal council of Hällefors has approved the annual report for Hällefors Bostads AB for the year 2025. The company reports a financial surplus of 1.4 million Swedish krona.

item.title: ON, Norberg Municipality, June 24, 2026.
item.description: The Care Committee of Norberg Municipality held a meeting on June 24, 2026. During the meeting, decisions were made regarding various matters, including finances, personnel, and plans for elderly care services.

item.title: Minutes from the Monetary Policy Meeting held on June 16, 2026.
item.description: The interest rate will remain unchanged. It will stay at 1.75 percent following a decision made on June 16, 2026.

item.title: ÖFN, Tranemo Municipality, June 24, 2026.
item.description: The municipality of Tranemo has changed the rules regarding who is authorized to make decisions in cases involving individuals requiring assistance (legal guardians). They have also decided not to provide funding to an organization that sup

item.title: The Swedish National Audit Office's report on the state's governance of municipalities' exercise of authority, specifically concerning the LSS (Law on Support and Service for Persons with Disabilities).
item.description: The Swedish National Audit Office has reviewed how the state ensures that municipalities comply with the LSS Act, which provides support to people with disabilities. They believe that the state's efforts are not effective enough and want to

item.title: Follow-up on the crime prevention strategy "Barriers Against Crime."
item.description: The government has conducted a follow-up on its plan to combat crime. They believe that the new methods are helping to reduce recruitment into criminal gangs.

item.title: Follow-up of the national strategy to combat organized crime.
item.description: The government has released a report detailing the efforts to combat gangs and organized crime in Sweden. The report states that the work in this area has improved, but that crime remains a significant threat to society.

item.title: Philip R. Lane: Introductory remarks.
item.description: The situation in the Middle East and high energy prices are creating uncertainty for Europe's economy. To protect the economy from rising prices, a slight interest rate increase has been implemented.

item.title: The Commission is simplifying and making energy labeling and tire labeling more accessible for European consumers.
item.description: The EU wants to make it easier for everyone to understand the labeling on tires and appliances. This is intended to help people make better choices when they are shopping.

item.title: The Commission plans to issue EU bonds with a value of 80 billion euros during the second half of 2026.
item.description: The EU plans to borrow 80 billion euros during the second half of 2026. This is part of a larger plan to borrow a total of 180 billion euros throughout the entire year.

item.title: Regarding the correction of the Estonian language version of Regulation (EU) No 10/2011 concerning plastic materials and articles intended to come into contact with foodstuffs.
item.description: A correction has been made to the Estonian text regarding EU regulations concerning plastics used for food. This correction does not change the regulations for other languages or countries.

item.title: COMMISSION IMPLEMENTING REGULATION (EU) 2026/1378
item.description: The EU has approved new regulations regarding the use of seeds from the Jatropha curcas plant as food. These seeds can now also be sold as flour and paste, and the amount that can be used in food products has been increased.

item.title: COMMISSION IMPLEMENTING REGULATION (EU) 2026/1376
item.description: The European Union has decided to allow the import of certain flowers from Uganda into Europe. However, there are strict regulations in place to prevent diseases and pests from being introduced along with the flowers.

item.title: Environment and Urban Planning Committee, Hässleholm Municipality, June 23, 2026.
item.description: The committee has decided to send a proposal for an amended detailed development plan for Norra Station in Bokeberg out for consultation. The proposal concerns making the regulations for parking spaces more flexible.

item.title: Signed document from Bun, Tibro municipality, June 23, 2026.
item.description: The Tibro Board of Education has made decisions regarding new regulations for childcare fees and has planned educational programs for high school students starting in 2026.

item.title: BUN, Municipality of Tjörn, June 23, 2026.
item.description: The Children and Education Committee of Tjöns municipality has reported that they currently have a surplus of 7 million Swedish krona in their budget.

item.title:
New political direction for a stronger film industry.
# A New Political Direction for a Stronger Film Industry
* **A clear vision:** We need a clear vision for the future of the film industry. * **Increased funding:** We need to increase funding for film production and distribution. * **Support for independent filmmakers:** We need to provide more support for independent filmmakers. * **Promotion of diversity and inclusion:** We need to promote diversity and inclusion in the film industry. * **International collaboration:** We need to strengthen international collaboration in the film industry.
This new political direction will help to create a stronger and more vibrant film industry in Sweden. It will also help to promote Swedish culture and values around the world.
We believe that this new political direction is essential for the future of the film industry. We are confident that it will help to create a more sustainable and successful industry for years to come.
item.description: The government's proposal outlines new goals for national film policy. Based on the national cultural policy objectives, the national film policy should contribute to the creation and distribution of

item.title: Christine Lagarde: Hearing before the Committee on Economic and Monetary Affairs at the European Parliament.
item.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) is raising interest rates to curb rising prices. This measure is being taken to protect the economy in the wake of the war in the Middle East, which has made energy more expensive.

item.title: COUNCIL IMPLEMENTING REGULATION (EU) 2026/1402
item.description: The EU has updated its list of individuals subject to sanctions due to the situation in South Sudan. This update aims to continue penalizing a specific person for obstructing peace and human rights.

item.title: COMMISSION IMPLEMENTING REGULATION (EU) 2026/1371
item.description: The EU is correcting an error in the regulations regarding the additional customs duties on fiberglass from Bahrain, Egypt, and Thailand. The purpose is to ensure that the descriptions of the products and their corresponding codes are accur

item.title: COMMISSION IMPLEMENTING REGULATION (EU) 2026/1373
item.description: The EU is introducing an additional tariff on the chemical 1,4-butanediol imported from China, Saudi Arabia, and the United States. This measure is being implemented to protect European companies from prices that are artificially low due to

item.title: COMMISSION IMPLEMENTING REGULATION (EU) 2026/1404
item.description: The EU is introducing new regulations for the import of poultry meat from the United States and Canada to protect animal health. Imports from certain regions of the United States are being halted due to new disease outbreaks, while imports

item.title: Leisure and Culture Committee, Arboga Municipality, June 22, 2026.
item.description: The Leisure and Culture Committee in Arboga has decided not to introduce new rules for scholarships. They believe it is too difficult to make assessments that are fair to everyone.

item.title: Municipal Council, Emmaboda Municipality, June 22, 2026.
item.description: The municipality of Emmaboda has made several important decisions regarding finances and organization. These include the municipality acting as a guarantor for a riding club and establishing a new subsidiary company for the glass industry.

item.title: Municipal Council, Markaryd Municipality, June 22, 2026.
item.description: At the municipal council meeting on June 22, 2026, several important issues regarding schools, finances, and security were discussed by the elected politicians. They also reviewed future budgets and the need for new equipment for the staff.

item.title: Elections Committee, Strängnäs Municipality, 2026-06-22.
item.description: The election committee in Strängnäs has been working on making changes to various political appointments. Several people are leaving their positions, and new individuals need to be elected to replace them in different committees and compani

item.title: Personnel Committee, Uddevalla Municipality, June 22, 2026.
item.description: The municipality of Uddevalla is updating its regulations regarding smoke-free workplaces to include modern products such as e-cigarettes. They are also removing some old and unnecessary rules concerning staff meetings and improvement sugge

item.title: Social Services Department, Vingaker Municipality, June 22, 2026.
item.description: The Social Services Board has decided how the responsibility for decisions regarding transportation services and other social issues will be distributed among different individuals within the municipality. They have also received a progress

item.title: Frank Elderson: A relaxed Q&A session.
item.description: Frank Elderson, from the European Central Bank (ECB), explains how banking regulations can be simplified without compromising the stability of banks. The goal is to strengthen Europe's economy and make it easier for banks to provide loans f

item.title: Piero Cipollone: The Foundations of National Sovereignty: The Role of the Central Bank in Monetary Policy.
item.description: Piero Cipollone, from the European Central Bank (ECB), states that Europe needs to become more independent in the realm of digital payments. To protect the euro and our economy, we must not become overly reliant on payment systems originati

item.title: The European Central Bank (ECB) will publish aggregated bank data for December 2025.
item.description: The European Central Bank has released new statistics showing the performance of banks in the EU as of December 2025. These figures help us understand the stability and profitability of the banks.

item.title: Bill: Tougher penalties for organised crime
item.description: The government proposes higher minimum sentences and expanded powers for covert surveillance in cases of organised crime.

item.title: Sweden's foreign intelligence service
item.description: Sweden is establishing a new authority – Sweden's Foreign Intelligence Service (UND) – which is to begin operating on 1 January 2027. The government proposes a new law on how the authority may process personal data, together with consequential amendments to more than twenty other laws so that they work together with the new authority.

item.title: Signed by ON, Ljusdal Municipality, June 18, 2026.
item.description: The Social Services Board of Ljusdal has adopted a new plan for how information and documents should be handled, stored, and disposed of. This plan applies to all documents, including those dating back to 2010 and beyond.

item.title: Municipal Board, Älvsbyn Municipality June 18, 2026
item.description: The municipality of Älvsbyn proposes to contribute to the financing of loans for a new, secure housing facility. The municipality will also prepare the land to enable the start of construction.

item.title: Municipal Council, Finspång Municipality, June 18, 2026.
item.description: The meeting in the municipality of Finspång covered topics including finances, healthcare, and changes to public transportation. Discussions also included future plans for housing and how to implement crime prevention measures in rural area

item.title: Service and Technology Committee, Katrineholm Municipality, June 18, 2026.
item.description: The Service and Technology Committee has approved the proposals for the municipality's budget for the years 2027-2029. They have also made a decision regarding revised prices for renting the municipality's premises and sports facilities, ef

item.title: SBN, Köping Municipality, 2026-06-18.
item.description: Sida 1 (16) Protokoll Beslutande organ Samhällsbyggnadsnämnden Mötesdatum 2026-06-18 Tid 09.00–10.50 Plats KS-salen, Rådhuset Närvarande Se nästa sida. Paragrafer 54–64 Justeringens plats och tid Samh

item.title: The Board of Guardianship, Härnösand, Kramfors, Kramfors Municipality, June 18, 2026.
item.description: The guardianship boards of Härnösand and Kramfors have decided on new rules regarding how decisions are made and how the work should be managed. They have also chosen to respond to an audit of their operations.

item.title: To: The Board of Guardianship, Kristianstad Municipality Date: 2026-06-18
item.description: The Board of Guardianship in Kristianstad has decided to change its rules regarding who is authorized to make decisions. These new rules will come into effect on July 1, 2026, to comply with new legislation.

item.title: ON, Ljusdal Municipality, June 18, 2026.
item.description: The Social Welfare Board in Ljusdal has approved a new plan for how to handle and store public documents. They have also reviewed information regarding the budget process for 2027 and summer staffing arrangements.

item.title: The Urban Planning Committee of the municipality of Växjö, June 18, 2026.
item.description: The Urban Planning Committee of the Växjö municipality has received several proposals from residents regarding various issues, including new street names, parking spaces, and traffic environments.

item.title: City Council, City of Stockholm, 2026-06-17
item.description: The City Council of Stockholm has responded to questions regarding traffic changes, addressed a legal review of the Slussen area, and provided feedback on proposals concerning how personal data can be used to prevent crime.
